title: Kafka Invalid Message Batch and Corrupt Record Errors
dataSources: Kafka broker log `sourcetype=kafka:serverLog` (record validation, CRC, and magic byte failures)
spl:
index=kafka sourcetype="kafka:serverLog"
| search "CorruptRecordException" OR "InvalidRecordException" OR "Record batch" OR "INVALID_RECORD" OR "checksum"
| rex "^\[[^\]]+\]\s+(?<log_level>\w+)"
| where log_level IN ("ERROR","WARN")
| stats count by host
| where count > 0
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable: this use case is not built on a CIM data model. Rely on the search in Step 2 against your raw logs or metrics.
description: Broker-side parse and checksum errors usually mean poison messages, interrupted log segments, or incompatible producer serializers.
value: Stops extended data corruption incidents from spreading across consumers and guides engineers to the offending topic/partition quickly.
grandmaExplanation: We use this to stops extended data corruption incidents from spreading across consumers and guides engineers to the offending topic/partition quickly.
implementation: Collect broker logs from all replicas. Include partition/topic regex extractions where your distribution logs them. Auto-open incidents when any broker exceeds zero errors in 5 minutes for tier-0 topics.
visualization: Timeline (corruption errors), Table (host, count), Single value (open error window).

references.title: Configure monitor inputs for the Splunk Add-on for Kafka

knownFalsePositives: Queues and broker metrics swing during rebalancing, replay, or maintenance. We align with change windows.
